title: Portable brinell testing apparatus
abstract: A portable Brinell testing apparatus that meets the load time requirements of the ASTM E1O standard. Prior art attempts do not work because as the force is applied, the indenter travels into the material causing the springs to extend therefore lowering the load applied. Material of different hardness cause the indenter to penetrate different amount. The invention uses a constant force spring connected to a lever arm which is pivoted on fulcrum (32). The mechanical advantage of the mechanism is able to achieve a force of up to 3000 kg with constant force spring (26) that can be activated by the operator.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1.) A Brinell testing apparatus for measuring the hardness of a test material positioned in said apparatus between an indenter in a holder and an elevated screw assembly such that said apparatus comprises: a lever arm in communication with the indenter in the holder wherein said lever arm has a fulcrum at one end and wherein said lever arm has an eccentric cam at the other end such that the mechanical advantage of said lever arm is able to achieve a force of up to 3000 kg such that the mechanical advantage of said lever arm is able to achieve a force of up to 3000 kg by rotating said eccentric cam; constant force spring attached to the end of said lever arm adjacent to said eccentric cam such that force being applied to the indenter remains constant despite the movement of the indenter into the test material during the required time period of the test. 2. The Brinell testing apparatus of Claim 1 further comprising a handle attached to said cam wherein a user pulling on said handle causes said cam to rotate thereby causing the force to be exerted on the indenter. 3. The Brinell testing apparatus of Claim 1 further comprising a battery operated electric motor which causes said cam to rotate thereby causing the force to be exerted on the indenter. 4. The Brinell testing apparatus of Claim 1 wherein said lever arm comprises a plurality of levers such that the mechanical advantage of said plurality of levers is greater than the mechanical advantage of a single lever arm. 1 5. ) The Brinell testing apparatus of Claim 1 further comprising an indicator that indicates when the desired force has been reached. 6. ) The Brinell testing apparatus of Claim 5 wherein said indicator is a pressure gauge. 7.) The Brinell testing apparatus of Claim 5 further comprising a load cell and an electronic display associated with said indicator to provide a continuous readout of the force being applied to the test material being measured 8.) The Brinell testing apparatus of Claim 5 further comprising a pressure transducer associated with an electronic display with said indicator providing a continuous read-out of the force being applied to the test material being measured. 2
